summary: No ship: the executor authenticates PR identity but not branch ownership, and the pagination witness can pass without exercising pagination.

findings.title: Blocker: PR authorship is treated as branch ownership
findings.body: `isTrusted` proves who opened a PR, but not who owns or last wrote its deterministic ref. Nevertheless, any remote ref without a visible PR is force-pushed by `adopt`, and a writer-authored PR remains trusted after another same-repo actor pushes to its head. A contents-write actor can therefore have commits erased by `adopt`, or leave its replacement head auto-merge-armed through `skip`. `--force-with-lease` only proves the OID stayed unchanged after observation; it provides no provenance. No writer-only rule for `bump-poll/**` is present.
findings.recommendation: Establish an enforceable ref-ownership invariant: reserve `bump-poll/**` for the writer App with a ruleset, or verify the observed head/tree against the locally prepared bump before every adopt/skip/rebuild and fail closed when provenance is unproven. Add witnesses for a foreign orphan ref and a writer-authored PR whose head was replaced.

findings.title: Test-quality: the fork-saturation witness never crosses a page boundary
findings.body: The stub wraps an arbitrarily large node array into one terminal GraphQL page. W11 supplies 200 forks plus the writer this way, although production requests `first:100`. The test therefore exercises filtering a large array, not aggregating later pages. An implementation that keeps `--paginate` but consumes only the first page can satisfy the current witnesses while missing a page-2 writer and recreating false absence, destructive adoption, or deployment suppression.
findings.recommendation: Inject a realistic multi-page `--slurp` envelope: page 1 with `hasNextPage:true`, one or more subsequent pages, and the trusted writer only on the terminal page. Require normal reconciliation and zero orphan misclassification.

findings.title: Accepted structure decisions are absent from the authoritative contract
findings.body: The plan still specifies bounded `gh pr list` discovery and says a non-writer same-repository PR leads to `create`. The implementation now intentionally uses exhaustive GraphQL discovery, `(head, base)` identity, Bot typing, fail-closed branch ownership, and bidirectional auto-merge reconciliation. The public tools README and increment result are similarly stale, while the plan's Review Decision Log contains no structure r1-r4 decisions. Future work and release review are therefore anchored to the pre-fix state machine and can fight or regress the accepted structure.
findings.recommendation: Synchronize the final state machine, GraphQL contract, trust rules, disarm behavior, witnesses, and structure decisions into the plan, increment result/state, and public README before the next gate.

next_steps: Fix and witness branch/ref ownership before shipping.
Replace the impossible single-page crowding fixture with a positive multi-page witness.
Update the authoritative plan and public contract to match the accepted implementation.
